examples.title: RGB Color Representation
examples.description: Demonstrates how RGB colors are represented and manipulated in Python
examples.explanation: Defines a class to represent RGB colors with value validation. Shows conversion to hex format commonly used in web and graphics applications.

examples.title: Linear Interpolation for Animation
examples.description: Implements linear interpolation (lerp) for generating tween frames
examples.explanation: Implements linear interpolation between two values and RGB colors. Used in animation for smooth transitions between keyframes. The t parameter controls position (0=start, 1=end).

examples.title: Ease-in-out Interpolation
examples.description: Implements easing functions for natural animation motion
examples.code:
import math

def ease_in_out(t):
    """Smooth acceleration and deceleration"""
    return 0.5 * (1 - math.cos(math.pi * t))

def ease_in(t):
    """Starts slow, accelerates"""
    return t * t

def ease_out(t):
    """Starts fast, decelerates"""
    return 1 - (1 - t) * (1 - t)

def linear(t):
    """Constant speed"""
    return t

# Generate animation frames
start_pos = 0
end_pos = 100
num_frames = 30

print("Frame positions with different easing:")
print("Frame\tLinear\tEaseIn\tEaseOut\tEaseInOut")
for i in range(num_frames + 1):
    t = i / num_frames
    linear_pos = start_pos + (end_pos - start_pos) * linear(t)
    ease_in_pos = start_pos + (end_pos - start_pos) * ease_in(t)
    ease_out_pos = start_pos + (end_pos - start_pos) * ease_out(t)
    ease_io_pos = start_pos + (end_pos - start_pos) * ease_in_out(t)
    print(f"{i:2d}\t{linear_pos:6.1f}\t{ease_in_pos:6.1f}\t{ease_out_pos:6.1f}\t{ease_io_pos:6.1f}")
examples.explanation: Demonstrates different easing functions for animation. Easing creates more natural motion than linear interpolation by simulating physics-based acceleration and deceleration.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of frames

examples.title: RGBA Color with Alpha Blending
examples.description: Implements alpha blending for transparent color compositing
examples.explanation: Implements alpha blending using standard compositing formula. When foreground color has alpha < 255, it blends with background based on transparency level. Used for UI, effects, and image compositing.
